sport_context.training_focus: Hypertrophy cutting split; 3 legs days per week; quad prioritization as weakest muscle group
sport_context.movement_patterns: Legs dominate the week (3 of 5 gym days); upper body Push/Pull; isolation + compound movements
sport_context.nutrition_strategy: High protein (2.2g/kg LBM); strict carb cycling around training blocks; iron-absorption pairing (vitamin C with iron sources)
sport_context.notes: Lower back: no heavy deadlifts, hyperextensions, loaded good mornings, RDLs. Knees: controlled ROM only — no ATG squats, no deep knee flexion beyond 90 degrees. Quads are Carol's weakest muscle group — prioritize quad volume on Legs A and Legs C.

constraints.lower_back: Moderate injury. No RDLs, no conventional deadlifts, no hyperextensions, no loaded good mornings. Hip hinge work limited to cable pull-throughs and machine/barbell hip thrusts (padded, supported). All rows are supported or cable-based.
constraints.knees: Mild pain. Controlled ROM only — no ATG squats, no deep knee flexion beyond 90 degrees on any squat pattern. Monitor all quad-dominant movements.
constraints.equipment_available: barbell, dumbbells, cables, leverage machines (leg press, hack squat, leg curl, calf raise), benches

progression.rule: When all working sets are completed at RPE 7 or below for two consecutive sessions, increase load by the smallest available increment (2.5 kg for barbells, 2 kg for dumbbells). Never exceed RPE 9 in the accumulation block.
progression.volume_approach: Legs dominate the week (3 days). Quad volume elevated on Legs A and Legs C. Glute/hamstring volume elevated on Legs B.

weekly_schedule.friday: Legs C — Full Legs Pump + Accessories

warmup_template.general: 5 min light cardio (bike or rowing machine — no treadmill running due to lower back protocol). Follow with 3–5 targeted mobility drills for the day's primary joints.
mobility_monday: Quad pull (knee to chest) 10 reps per side
Leg swing forward 10 reps per side
mobility_wednesday: Leg swing lateral 10 reps per side

sessions.scheduling_note: Morning session. M1 pre-workout meal 30–45 min before gym. Knee protocol active: max 90° knee flexion on all squat patterns. Lower back protocol active.

metabolic_primer.name: AMRAP Quad Dominant Activation
metabolic_primer.protocol: As many rounds as possible in 8 min: 12 × Bodyweight Squat (90° hard cap) + 10 × Glute Bridge + 10 × Banded Clamshell per side
metabolic_primer.instructions: Strict 90° knee angle on every squat rep — do NOT go deeper. Glute bridge: 2s hold at the top, feel glutes contract, NOT the lower back. Banded clamshell protects the knee joint. Move continuously between exercises with minimal rest. This primes the quads and glutes before loading.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.note: Primary quad compound. Feet hip-width on mid-platform. Lower to exactly 90° knee angle — do not push deeper (knee protocol). Keep lower back pressed firmly into the pad. Do not lock out at the top. 2s eccentric.

exercises.name: Hack Squat
exercises.note: 90° ROM hard cap — do not go deeper. Feet slightly wider than shoulder-width, toes slightly out. Drive through the full foot — do not shift onto the toes. Brace the core deliberately throughout. Excellent for quad emphasis over glutes.

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.note: Pure quad isolation — the seated position keeps the lumbar spine completely unloaded. 2s concentric, 3s eccentric. Full extension at the top — do not hyperextend the knees. Prioritize mind-muscle connection here; this is Carol's weakest muscle group.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat
exercises.note: Hold dumbbell at chest level (goblet position). Feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out. Lower to exactly 90° knee angle — do not go deeper. The goblet position naturally pulls the chest up and prevents lumbar hyperextension. 2s eccentric. Use moderate weight — this is an anterior-chain primer, not a max-effort lift.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.note: Full stretch at the bottom (slow controlled descent), explosive drive up, 1s hold at peak. Calves respond well to high reps and time under tension. Vary foot position: toes neutral for 2 sets, toes slightly out for 1 set, toes slightly in for 1 set.

sessions.scheduling_note: Morning session. Full upper back and biceps volume day. All rows are supported or cable-based per lower back protocol.

metabolic_primer.name: AMRAP Posterior Shoulder Circuit
metabolic_primer.protocol: 3-exercise circuit — complete as many rounds as possible in 7 minutes: 10 × Band Pull-Apart + 10 × Cable Face Pull (light) + 10 × Machine Row (very light load)
metabolic_primer.instructions: Move with intent and short transitions. This primes the posterior shoulder and upper back before loading. Scale machine row weight to something that feels like RPE 5 — this is activation, not a working set. No explosive movements or lower body involvement.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (Wide Grip)

warmup_sets.note: Focus on lat depression before pulling
Build lat engagement — feel the stretch at the top

exercises.note: Initiate every rep by depressing the scapulae before the arms move. Pull to upper chest. 2s concentric, 2s eccentric. Never pull behind the neck. This is Tuesday's primary back movement.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row (Close Grip)
exercises.note: Brace the core before each rep. Slight lean back at the contraction is fine — do NOT hyperextend the lumbar spine. Drive elbows back and squeeze the shoulder blades together at the peak. 2s eccentric.

exercises.name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Row (Supported on Bench)
exercises.note: Bracing arm and knee on the bench removes lumbar load entirely — critical for lower back protocol. Keep hips squared (do not rotate to swing the weight). Drive elbow high and back. 2s eccentric. Perform both sides — 3 sets per side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bicep Curl (Alternating)
exercises.note: Supinate the wrist fully at the top of every rep to maximize bicep peak contraction. Do not swing — elbows stay pinned at sides. Full extension at the bottom on every rep.

exercises.name: Cable Hammer Curl
exercises.note: Neutral (hammer) grip throughout. Targets the brachialis, which adds arm thickness. Keep elbows pinned. Great complement to the supinated dumbbell curl.

sessions.scheduling_note: Morning session. Knee protocol: max 90° on all knee-flexion movements. Lower back protocol: no RDLs, deadlifts from floor, hyperextensions, or loaded good mornings. Hip hinge via cable pull-through and hip thrust only.

metabolic_primer.name: AMRAP Glute and Hamstring Activation
metabolic_primer.protocol: As many rounds as possible in 8 min: 15 × Glute Bridge (bodyweight) + 10 × Banded Clamshell per side + 10 × Bodyweight Squat (90° ROM hard cap)
metabolic_primer.instructions: Focus on mind-muscle connection during glute bridge — feel the glutes contracting, NOT the lower back. Banded clamshell activates hip abductors which protect the knee joint. Bodyweight squat depth is hard-capped at 90° (knee protocol). No jumping at any point.

exercises.name: Hip Thrust (Machine or Barbell)

warmup_sets.note: Use barbell pad if using barbell. Confirm neutral spine at the top before loading.

exercises.note: Upper back on a flat bench. Drive through the heel. Squeeze the glutes hard at the top and hold for 1 second. Critical: the spine does NOT extend at the top — ribs stay down, spine stays neutral. Machine hip thrust is preferred over barbell for lower back safety. 2s eccentric.

exercises.name: Cable Pull-Through
exercises.note: Attach a rope to the low pulley. Stand facing away from the machine, straddle the cable. Hinge at the hip — push the hips back until you feel hamstring tension, then drive the hips forward and squeeze the glutes. Zero spinal compression — this is the ideal hip hinge for Carol's lower back protocol. Do not hyperextend at the top. 2s eccentric.

exercises.name: Lying Leg Curl
exercises.note: Plantarflex the foot (point toes down) to optimize hamstring recruitment. 2s eccentric. Do not let the hips rise off the pad — hips rising means the lower back is compensating. The lying position is safe as long as hips stay neutral.

exercises.name: Seated Leg Curl
exercises.note: Seated leg curl hits the hamstring at a longer muscle length, targeting a different portion of the strength curve than the lying variation. 2s concentric, 3s eccentric. Keep hips against the pad. This deliberate variation maximizes hamstring recruitment across the full ROM.

exercises.name: Cable Kickback
exercises.note: Attach an ankle cuff to the low pulley. Face the machine and kick the leg straight back, squeezing the glute at the top. Keep the standing leg slightly bent. 2s concentric, 2s eccentric. This isolates the glute max without any lumbar stress. Perform both legs — 3 sets per side.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.note: Full stretch at the bottom (slow controlled descent), explosive drive up, 1s hold at the top. Vary foot position across sets: toes neutral for 2 sets, toes slightly out for 1 set, toes slightly in for 1 set.

sessions.scheduling_note: Morning session. Shoulder press is the primary strength movement today.

metabolic_primer.name: Tabata Band Shoulder Circuit
metabolic_primer.protocol: 20s work / 10s rest × 8 rounds alternating between Resistance Band Overhead Press and Resistance Band Lateral Raise
metabolic_primer.instructions: Use a light resistance band — this is shoulder pre-activation, not a working set. Move continuously with good tempo. No jumping or lower body explosive involvement. The goal is blood flow and shoulder joint preparation before the seated press.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press

warmup_sets.note: Movement pattern rehearsal — slow and controlled
Feel the chest stretch at the bottom
Build intent for the working sets

exercises.note: Lower bar to mid-chest, not the neck. Drive shoulders into the bench throughout the press. 2s eccentric — do not drop the bar. If any shoulder impingement discomfort, substitute with Dumbbell Bench Press.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press

warmup_sets.note: Confirm full ROM before adding load

exercises.note: Seated position eliminates lumbar load. Lower dumbbells to ear level, press to just short of lockout. Do not lean back excessively — keep torso upright. This is the primary strength lift for Thursday.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Press
exercises.note: 30-degree incline only — avoid steep inclines that stress the shoulder joint. 2s eccentric. Control the path: elbows at 45 degrees to the torso, not flared.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.note: Set the cable pulley low. Lead with the elbow, not the wrist. Stop at shoulder height — do not raise above the head. Slight forward lean is acceptable. Perform both sides — 3 sets per side.

exercises.name: Cable Tricep Pushdown (Rope Attachment)
exercises.note: Keep elbows pinned at sides throughout. Flare the rope handles at the bottom of each rep and pause briefly to maximize tricep contraction. Do not lean forward to add momentum.

sessions.focus: Legs C — Full Legs Pump + Accessories
sessions.scheduling_note: Morning session. Knee protocol: max 90° on all knee-flexion movements. Lower back protocol: hip hinge via cable only. This is the week's highest leg volume session — push the pump here.

metabolic_primer.name: Tabata Lower Body Pump Circuit
metabolic_primer.protocol: 20s work / 10s rest × 8 rounds alternating between Stationary Bodyweight Squat (90° hard cap) and Glute Bridge
metabolic_primer.instructions: Alternate each round: Round 1 bodyweight squat, Round 2 glute bridge, repeat. Maintain strict 90° knee angle on every squat rep — this is the hardest discipline under fatigue. Glute bridge: drive through the heel, squeeze glutes at the top. Keep transitions short. No jumping.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.note: Feet hip-width on mid-platform. Lower to exactly 90° knee angle — do not push deeper (knee protocol). Keep lower back pressed into the pad throughout. This is Legs C's first compound — go heavy but controlled. 2s eccentric.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.note: Rear foot elevated on bench (or step). Lower until the front knee reaches exactly 90° — do not go deeper (knee protocol). Drive through the front heel. Keep torso upright. Unilateral loading corrects left/right imbalances and is excellent for quad emphasis. Perform both legs — 3 sets per side.

exercises.name: Hip Thrust (Machine or Barbell)

warmup_sets.note: Use barbell pad if using barbell

exercises.note: Second hip thrust session of the week — glute training frequency is doubled intentionally for hypertrophy. Same form cues: drive through the heel, neutral spine at the top, 1s squeeze at peak. Machine hip thrust preferred for lower back safety.

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.note: Pure quad isolation — the seated position keeps the lumbar spine completely unloaded. 2s concentric, 3s eccentric. Full extension at the top — do not hyperextend. High-rep pump work here on Legs C to maximize quad hypertrophy. Carol's weakest muscle group — maximize time under tension.

exercises.name: Cable Adduction
exercises.note: Attach an ankle cuff to the low pulley. Stand sideways to the machine and swing the free leg across the body, squeezing the adductors at the peak. 2s concentric, 2s eccentric. The adductors are a neglected muscle group that contributes to overall leg development. Perform both legs — 3 sets per side.

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise
exercises.note: Seated position targets the soleus (a deeper calf muscle) rather than the gastrocnemius. Full stretch at the bottom, drive up with control, 1s hold at the top. Seated calf raises complete the lower leg development for the week.
